name: full-project
description: Full MAS RTL project: MAS → RTL → TB → SIM loop → DOC

tasks.detail: Write <module>_mas.md with all 9 sections: §1 Overview, §2 Module Hierarchy + Ports + Parameters, §3 Feature Operation + FSM, §4 Registers (FAM — offset/name/width/access/reset), §5 Interrupt (sources/enable/status/clear), §6 Memory (instances/type/depth/latency), §7 Timing, §8 RTL Notes, §9 DV Plan (Test Sequence table S1-SN + coverage goals + SVA assertions). This is the single source of truth for rtl_gen and tb_gen.

Read <module>_mas.md. Implement: ports from §2, FSM from §3, register map from §4, interrupt logic from §5, memory instances from §6. Follow §8 RTL Notes (nonblocking FF, no latches, sync reset). Use /gen-rtl <module_name>. Output: <module>.sv. Gate: lint 0 errors.
Run lint on <module>.sv. Fix all errors. Hard gate — tb_gen cannot start until clean.

tasks.content: [TB] Generate testbench guided by MAS §9 DV Plan: tb_<module>.sv + tc_<module>.sv
tasks.detail: Read <module>_mas.md §9. Implement every sequence S1-SN from the Test Sequence table. Add SVA assertions listed in coverage goals. Cover interrupt flow (S3), memory R/W (S4), error injection (S6). Use /gen-tb <module_name>. Output: tb_<module>.sv, tc_<module>.sv.
Run simulation. Fix bugs in DUT (rtl_gen) or TB (tb_gen) as needed. All S1-SN sequences must pass. Loop until 0 errors, 0 warnings.
Use /gen-doc <module_name>. Base on <module>_mas.md + simulation results. Include port table, register map, interrupt table, timing, simulation coverage summary. Output: <module>_spec.md.
